postLog.postText: Quick question for dental practice owners ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” how many new patient calls are you missing after 5pm and on weekends? Most practices lose 30-40% of inbound calls to voicemail. We built an AI receptionist that answers every call 24/7, sounds completely human, and books patients directly on your calendar. Call it right now: +1 (325) 240-6238

PI attorneys ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” what happens when someone gets in an accident at 9pm on a Friday and calls your firm? If they hit voicemail, they call the next firm on Google. We built an AI receptionist that picks up every call 24/7, qualifies the lead, and books a consult. Call it: +1 (325) 240-6238

Property managers and multifamily investors ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” how many leasing inquiries are you missing after hours? Every missed call is a vacancy that stays empty longer. We set up an AI receptionist that answers 24/7, schedules showings, and logs maintenance requests. Test it: +1 (325) 240-6238

Office managers ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” quick question. When your front desk is slammed between 8 and 10am and the phone won't stop ringing, what happens to the calls that don't get answered?

Most practices I've talked to are losing new patient calls during their busiest hours ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” not after hours. The front desk is checking patients in, verifying insurance, handling the waiting room. The phone rings. Nobody gets to it.

We've been setting up an AI receptionist that handles overflow calls during peak hours and picks up everything after close. Sounds completely human, books patients on the calendar, handles new patient FAQs.

Call it like you're a new patient calling during a busy Tuesday morning: +1 (325) 240-6238

Curious how other office managers are handling call volume right now ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” what's your system?

PI attorneys ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” genuine question. What's your after-hours intake process right now?

I've been talking to a lot of firms and the answers range from "answering service that costs $800/month and misses half the nuance" to "honestly, voicemail and we call back in the morning." Neither is great when your client called right after an accident and needs to feel taken care of.

We've built an AI receptionist that does 24/7 intake ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” gathers the basics, qualifies the lead, books the consult, and makes the caller feel heard. Zero overseas call centers. Sounds like your own intake specialist.

Want to hear it? Call and pitch it a case: +1 (325) 240-6238

What are people here actually using for after-hours? I'm genuinely curious what the standard is.

Rental operators ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” quick question. When someone calls at 11pm trying to book a car for tomorrow morning, what happens on your end?

Most private rental operators I talk to say the same thing: the call goes to voicemail, and by the time they call back in the morning the customer already found someone else or booked on Turo.

We've been setting up an AI receptionist for private rental operators that picks up every call instantly ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” sounds exactly like your own staff, checks availability, locks in the reservation, and handles the whole conversation start to finish. No call centers. No overseas answering service.

Call it like you're trying to book a car last minute: +1 (325) 240-6238

Curious how other operators here are handling late-night and weekend inquiries ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” is it mostly voicemail or do you have something else set up?

Plumbers ГўВЂВ” honest question. How many calls a day are going to voicemail while your crew is on jobs?

For most shops I talk to it's 3ГўВЂВ“5 a day minimum. Each one is a potential $800ГўВЂВ“$3,000 job that called the next contractor on Google while you were finishing the one in front of you.

We've built an AI receptionist for plumbing businesses that handles every inbound call ГўВЂВ” books the job, answers questions about pricing and availability, and triages emergency calls vs standard scheduling. Sounds completely human. Your customers won't know the difference.

Want to hear it? Call this number and pitch it a slab leak: +1 (325) 240-6238

How are others here handling call volume when the whole crew is tied up? Curious what systems people are actually using.

Long Island business owners вЂ” quick question about your phones.

What happens when someone calls your business at 7pm on a Friday trying to book an appointment or get a quick answer? For most local businesses it goes to voicemail, and they move on to whoever picks up.

I've been working with service businesses here on Long Island on an AI receptionist that picks up every call 24/7 вЂ” sounds like a real person on your team, books appointments, handles the usual questions without you having to be near a phone. The owners who've tested it are usually surprised how natural it sounds.

Want to hear it for yourself? Give it a call: +1 (325) 240-6238

Genuinely curious how LI business owners here are handling this вЂ” voicemail, answering service, or something else?
